weekly_observations: Fifth straight week with no entries; the gap-up trap pattern (RSI 65-83 at open, sharp reversal by midday) fired every single day across PANW, MGM, ES, IBKR, and AMD. Cash at 0.00% outperformed SPY on each day data was available (Mon -0.43%, Tue -0.96%, Wed -0.24%), adding approximately +1.63pts of alpha through pure restraint. The core diagnosis is clear: the current 4-6 name watchlist is structurally broken — chronic ATR% compression (0.05-0.23% vs 0.30 floor) and repeat gap-up failures mean no qualifying entries exist. The critical priority for next week is a full universe refresh using ATR% >0.35% as the primary screening filter before any other criterion, coupled with pre-market gap-size screening to flag names with >2.5% overnight moves as elevated-gap-risk. The RSI 55-60 ceiling and ATR% >=0.30% entry gates are non-negotiable and proved their value again this week; the issue is the candidate pool, not the filters.
